title: Rednecks And Broomsticks

jokes.text: How can you call it a ski weekend if we're going home at dawn on Sunday?
jokes.setup: Family is on a ski weekend trip
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the absurdly short duration of their 'weekend'
jokes.explanation: Classic Lisa rationality pointing out family dysfunction - well-structured observational humor

jokes.text: I hate traffic: the band and the phenomenon.
jokes.setup: Homer expressing frustration about being stuck in traffic
jokes.punchline: Clarifying he hates both the traffic jam AND the rock band Traffic
jokes.explanation: Clever dual meaning joke with excellent economy - the kind of layered humor Simpsons excels at

jokes.text: There's only one game we can play in the car over and over and over again: Bonk it.
jokes.setup: Need for car entertainment, buildup suggesting limited options
jokes.punchline: Reveals annoying electronic game (parody of Bop It)
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the coming chaos - establishes the annoying toy that will torture Homer

jokes.text: No problem. A couple just rolled out from behind your seat.
jokes.setup: Marge says they lost the batteries for the toy
jokes.punchline: Bart finds random batteries in the messy car, implying Homer's slovenliness
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor about Homer's messiness enabling the kids' torture

jokes.text: Bonk it. Twist it. Smack it. Thwack it. Whack it. Bonk it. Bop it.
jokes.setup: The annoying electronic toy game begins
jokes.punchline: The commands become increasingly violent-sounding and repetitive
jokes.explanation: Basic escalation joke that relies on the inherent annoyance of repetitive electronic toys

jokes.text: I can't take it anymore!
jokes.setup: Continuous annoying electronic game sounds
jokes.punchline: Homer's breaking point reached very quickly
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer breaking point moment - functional but predictable

jokes.text: Oh, darn. The batteries are dead. Not to worry: it plugs into the cigarette lighter.
jokes.setup: Hope that the toy is finally dead
jokes.punchline: False relief - the torture can continue indefinitely
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - gives hope then crushes it, classic torture comedy escalation

jokes.text: Stomp it! Crush it! Kill it!
jokes.setup: Homer takes his turn with the game
jokes.punchline: Commands become increasingly violent, showing his rage
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Homer's violent frustration through increasingly destructive commands

jokes.text: Why do my actions have consequences?
jokes.setup: Homer presumably crashes the car after his violent game-playing
jokes.punchline: Homer's childish inability to understand cause and effect
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer line - encapsulates his entire worldview in one quotable sentence about avoiding responsibility

jokes.text: Drink up, sweetie. Let the possum work its magic. Possum?! Don't worry, we don't kill it. We just dip it.
jokes.setup: Offering Lisa a drink to warm up
jokes.punchline: Reveals the 'tea' is made by dipping a live possum in hot water
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor - the casual reassurance about 'just dipping' the possum makes it even funnier

jokes.text: Where's the hooch? - Excuse my faux pas. That jug was fo' pa.
jokes.setup: Homer asking for moonshine
jokes.punchline: Cletus makes a pun on 'faux pas' and 'fo' pa' (for father)
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows unexpected sophistication from the hillbilly character

jokes.text: He's gonna shoot those google earth folks what caught me with my breeches down.
jokes.setup: Brandine explaining why they need the bazooka
jokes.punchline: Modern paranoia about Google Earth satellite imagery catching embarrassing moments
jokes.explanation: Smart cultural reference joke about modern technology invading rural privacy

jokes.text: A female that gives birth to alcohol.
jokes.setup: Cletus introducing his moonshine still named 'ol betsy'
jokes.punchline: Describing the still in biological/reproductive terms
jokes.explanation: Good character line that shows Cletus's folksy way of describing his equipment

jokes.text: It angrys up my fists! I just remembered my feud with this here tree!
jokes.setup: Cletus tasting the moonshine
jokes.punchline: Moonshine makes him irrationally angry at inanimate objects, inventing verb 'angrys'
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor with great made-up grammar 'angrys up' and ridiculous tree feud

jokes.text: It's got a rich mash base and a sense of danger. And I detect notes of elderberry, tobacco, oak... No, poison oak... and hints of game.
jokes.setup: Homer doing a sophisticated wine-tasting style analysis
jokes.punchline: Analysis escalates from pretentious to alarming (poison oak, game)
jokes.explanation: Great subversion of wine-tasting pretension with increasingly disturbing 'notes' detected

jokes.text: You have the eloquence and urgency of a census worker caught in a bear trap.
jokes.setup: Cletus responding to Homer's moonshine review
jokes.punchline: Bizarre but vivid metaphor combining bureaucracy with wilderness danger
jokes.explanation: Exceptionally creative and specific metaphor that's both absurd and weirdly accurate - peak Simpsons writing

jokes.text: Nope, but my mama brought some of these boom potatoes back from Iraq.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking about video games
jokes.punchline: Reveals they play with actual grenades from military deployment
jokes.explanation: Dark military humor with the innocent term 'boom potatoes' for grenades

jokes.text: One. Two. Backwards 'e'. One-legged triangle. Banana hot dog. Double banana hot dog. 60 corncob two.
jokes.setup: Kids counting for hide and seek
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical counting system showing their poor education
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist take on rural education deficit - creative nonsense numbers

jokes.text: Why did I do poetry club instead of girl scouts?
jokes.setup: Lisa lost and alone in the woods
jokes.punchline: Regretting her intellectual choices over practical survival skills
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - her intellectual pursuits vs practical needs

jokes.text: You are going to be so embarrassed. There are two other people at this party dressed just like you.
jokes.setup: Lisa encounters hooded figures in the woods
jokes.punchline: Treats them like party guests with unfortunate fashion coincidences
jokes.explanation: Great subversion of scary encounter into mundane social situation

jokes.text: Technically... but we're not into broomsticks or pointy hats.
jokes.setup: Lisa asks if they're witches
jokes.punchline: Modern wiccans distance themselves from Halloween stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about modern paganism vs pop culture witches.

jokes.text: If magic was real, I'd use it to get out of my pipe cleaner art project that's due tomorrow.
jokes.setup: Witches claiming their spells work
jokes.punchline: Lisa's pragmatic skepticism focuses on immediate school concerns
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Lisa's practical priorities even when faced with the supernatural

jokes.text: What are you talking about? - Text your mom to pick us up.
jokes.setup: Dramatic mystical exit declaration
jokes.punchline: Immediately deflated by mundane modern logistics
jokes.explanation: Excellent deflation of mystical pretension with modern teen reality

jokes.text: Class, miss hoover will not be in today, so you all get a's on your pipe cleaner sculptures.
jokes.setup: Lisa's earlier wish to get out of her pipe cleaner project
jokes.punchline: The 'spell' works - Miss Hoover is sick and assignment is cancelled
jokes.explanation: Good callback to establish the 'magic working' premise for Lisa's story arc

jokes.text: Came down with a spell of stomach flu. Spell?
jokes.setup: Teacher explaining Miss Hoover's absence
jokes.punchline: Lisa catches the inadvertent pun on 'spell' given recent witch encounter
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay that advances Lisa's growing belief in the witches

jokes.text: Which craft? Which craft?!
jokes.setup: Substitute asking Lisa to pick a craft project
jokes.punchline: Lisa hears 'witchcraft' and becomes increasingly agitated
jokes.explanation: Good escalating paranoia moment with simple but effective pun

jokes.text: There's something wonderful about being drunk outdoors.
jokes.setup: Characters enjoying moonshine in nature
jokes.punchline: Barney's alcoholic perspective on the beauty of outdoor drinking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barney line - finds the poetic in his alcoholism

jokes.text: I wonder if Moe misses us. - No, I don't miss you. Now, get back to my bar for happy hour, or I'll paint the snow pink with your brains.
jokes.setup: Homer wondering if their bartender misses them
jokes.punchline: Cut to Moe threatening them with graphic violence if they don't return
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons cut-away joke with Moe's characteristic violent imagery

jokes.text: You know, one hillbilly has his way with one fat guy in deliverance, and suddenly, people think that's all hillbillies do. I suppose you think all space is Star Wars.
jokes.setup: Homer assuming sexual assault from hillbillies
jokes.punchline: Cletus's meta-commentary on stereotyping with absurd analogy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about movie stereotypes with perfect absurd comparison

jokes.text: You'll never know what you missed out on.
jokes.setup: Homer's sexual advance being rejected
jokes.punchline: Homer's petulant response to rejection
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer sexual inappropriateness - character-appropriate but not exceptional

jokes.text: But if the goddess chose to help you by giving miss Hoover the hershey squirts, such is her divine Wisdom.
jokes.setup: Explaining they wouldn't hurt a teacher
jokes.punchline: Crude bathroom humor juxtaposed with mystical language
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between spiritual pretension and crude reality

jokes.text: That's smart thinkin'! - Shut up and dig your own grave.
jokes.setup: Lisa's reasonable decision-making being praised
jokes.punchline: Immediate shift to death threat undermines the praise
jokes.explanation: Good whiplash comedy - praise immediately contradicted by violence

jokes.text: Savor the steps leading up to it. College anorexic, string of bad marriages, career disappointments, failed pottery shop.
jokes.setup: Lisa wanting to become a witch
jokes.punchline: Marge's cynical roadmap of female life disappointments
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy about women's life trajectories - specific and cutting observations

jokes.text: I'm gonna put a spell on you! Yes, I am, My tuna-breath familiar. Zap! Zap! Zap! I curse you, hell's kitten!
jokes.setup: Lisa playing witch with Santa's Little Helper
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top mystical language applied to family dog with halitosis
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment with Lisa's mystical posturing and mundane pet reality

jokes.text: Her buddhism has led directly to witchcraft. And she's teleconferencing the devil using that computer from hell! I think it says 'Dell.'
jokes.setup: Homer's panic about Lisa's spiritual exploration
jokes.punchline: Mishears 'Dell' computer brand as 'hell,' Marge's practical correction
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay with Homer's paranoia and Marge's practical deflation

jokes.text: Toss them a Judy Blume novel, Lou. Uh, all we've got is Wifey, chief. Nothing with kids in it? Damn budget cuts.
jokes.setup: Police feeling sorry for arrested witches
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate adult novel due to police department budget issues
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist bureaucracy humor about inappropriate police resources

jokes.text: We're not the witches! Oh, well then you must be victims of the witches' hideous curse.
jokes.setup: Reporter commenting on disfigured people
jokes.punchline: Mistake ugly protesters for cursed victims instead of witches
jokes.explanation: Visual gag about ugly protesters being mistaken for supernatural victims

jokes.text: You didn't think I was so hideous that night we made out in the back booth at the seafood hut. We all did crazy things on 9/11.
jokes.setup: Protester defending their appearance to reporter
jokes.punchline: Reporter's cruel dismissal using 9/11 as excuse for poor judgment
jokes.explanation: Dark humor using national tragedy as excuse for personal poor decisions

jokes.text: I'm blind! I'm blind! What goes around, comes around.
jokes.setup: Witches casting blindness spell
jokes.punchline: Karma immediately affecting the cruel reporter, Lisa's knowing comment
jokes.explanation: Good karmic justice moment with immediate consequences for cruelty

jokes.text: I'm blind, too! Then I guess it's up to me.
jokes.setup: Medical emergency with blind doctor
jokes.punchline: Nurse also blind, Dr. Hibbert's inappropriate confidence
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of medical incompetence - Dr. Hibbert's characteristic overconfidence

jokes.text: My real name isn't even Gwendolyn Nightshadow. It's Stacey, Stacey Deathsatan.
jokes.setup: Revelation that witch name is fake
jokes.punchline: Real name is even more ridiculously gothic
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - expected teen reality but gets even more absurd goth name

jokes.text: I am a big dummy with a stupid job. I write down what other people say, just like a big dummy would.
jokes.setup: Courtroom proceeding with magical influence
jokes.punchline: Court reporter's brutal self-assessment of their profession
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor about the absurdity of certain professions

jokes.text: Are you sure? 'cause we've brought you a lot of flimsy cases. Like that goat we accused of income tax evasion. I still think he was guilty.
jokes.setup: Judge commenting on flimsy case
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's examples of even more absurd previous cases
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist humor about police incompetence with specific ridiculous example

jokes.text: And now I can really hear how I bomb!
jokes.setup: Comedian going blind from the spell
jokes.punchline: Finding silver lining in not having to see audience's negative reactions
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about performer's self-awareness and unexpected benefits of disability

jokes.text: If the alleged witches survive the dunking, They clearly were witches! If they drown, then they were clearly innocent.
jokes.setup: Explaining dunking stool logic
jokes.punchline: Win-win situation for accusers, lose-lose for accused
jokes.explanation: Historical witch trial logic accurately portrayed but not particularly creative. Educational more than funny.

jokes.text: Good seats still available. By which I mean, seats in the back where you can fall asleep.
jokes.setup: Promoting school play tickets
jokes.punchline: Honest admission that the play will be boring
jokes.explanation: Good Skinner character moment - unintentionally honest about school production quality

jokes.text: Wait, wait, wait, don't unform! You're a great mob! We'll think of something else to get upset about! How about all that construction on route one, huh?
jokes.setup: Mob disbanding after witches cleared
jokes.punchline: Desperate attempt to maintain mob energy with mundane traffic complaints
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-commentary on mob mentality and the banality of actual complaints

jokes.text: Oh, you ain't no mob. You're just a bunch of spineless weasels. Hey, hey, hey, it's just a figure of speech. Kill the weasel hater!
jokes.setup: Mob leader insulting the disbanding crowd
jokes.punchline: Accidentally creates new mob target through poor word choice
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how easily mobs redirect their anger at anything

jokes.text: Cool! Me, Lisa Simpson!
jokes.setup: Witches calling Lisa cool
jokes.punchline: Lisa's amazement at being considered cool for once
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - her shock at social acceptance

jokes.text: The only 'witch' in lisa's life is which boy will marry her. Right, Homer? Homer?
jokes.setup: Marge trying to end Lisa's witch phase
jokes.punchline: Awkward pun followed by realization Homer has disappeared
jokes.explanation: Weak pun followed by standard 'where's Homer' beat - functional but unimpressive

tmdb_overview: Lisa befriends three teenaged Wiccans after getting lost in the woods during a game of hide-and-seek, and must clear her new friends' names when they are accused of cursing the townspeople with their supposed witchcraft. Meanwhile, Homer befriends Cletus after learning that he and his friends make their own moonshine.
